title: Quantum Startup Boom Draws New Money — and New Scrutiny
body:
The past two months have made clear that quantum computing is no longer a science project in the eyes of serious capital allocators. Volta Systems' $400 million close, anchored by Apex Ventures, is the largest single round in the European quantum sector on record.

But with large raises come large expectations — and, increasingly, regulatory attention. Volta's move to acquire Dutch photonics supplier LuminCore has already triggered a preliminary EU competition review, a development that some in the industry see as a template for how Brussels will treat consolidation in emerging tech sectors.

The company's CEO, Rolf Andersen, navigated a difficult few weeks: announcing a major hire in Dr. Elena Vasquez — poached from IBM Research — and almost simultaneously announcing an acquisition that put his company under a regulatory microscope.

Andersen told one investor briefing, a transcript of which was shared with this publication, that he views the regulatory scrutiny as "manageable" and expects clearance within the standard review window. He did not say what contingency plans exist if the deal is blocked.

Meanwhile, Apex managing partner Diana Chu has been visible in the press, positioning the investment as a long-term infrastructure bet. Sources say Apex has already begun due diligence on a second quantum-adjacent company, though no deal is imminent.
